repo.description: The most widely used Python to C compiler
repo.name: cython

comment_data.body:
I'd be happy if the item type of comprehensions could be found by type inference rather than requiring users to provide a declaration.

(EDIT: This is now https://github.com/cython/cython/issues/7684)
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1976,8 +1976,10 @@ def _generate_module_array_traverse_and_clear(self, struct_attr_cname, count, ma
         writer.putln(f"for ({counter_type} i=0; i<{count}; ++i) {{ Py_CLEAR(clear_module_state->{struct_attr_cname}[i]); }}")
 
     def generate_object_constant_decls(self):
-        consts = [(len(c.cname), c.cname, c)
-                  for c in self.arg_default_constants]
+        consts: list[tuple] = [
+            (len(c.cname), c.cname, c)
+                for c in self.arg_default_constants
+        ]

comment_data.body:
While C `int` is probably sufficient in practice, there is no reason (that I can see) to restrict users to an arbitrary limit.
```suggestion
    cdef Py_ssize_t bracket_nesting_level
```
The same applies to the usages and related method arguments.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -55,5 +55,16 @@ cdef class PyrexScanner(Scanner):
 
 
 cdef class FTStringState:
-    cdef list bracket_states
+    cdef list[FTStringBracketState] bracket_states
     cdef readonly str scanner_state
+
+    cdef bracket_nesting_level(self)
+    cdef bint in_format_specifier(self)
+    cdef set_in_format_specifier(self)
+    cdef push_bracket_state(self, int bracket_nesting_level)
+    cdef pop_bracket_state(self)
+
+@cython.final
+cdef class FTStringBracketState:
+    cdef int bracket_nesting_level

@@ -55,5 +55,16 @@ cdef class PyrexScanner(Scanner):
 
 
 cdef class FTStringState:
-    cdef list bracket_states
+    cdef list[FTStringBracketState] bracket_states
     cdef readonly str scanner_state
+
+    cdef bracket_nesting_level(self)
+    cdef bint in_format_specifier(self)
+    cdef set_in_format_specifier(self)
+    cdef push_bracket_state(self, int bracket_nesting_level)
+    cdef pop_bracket_state(self)
+
+@cython.final
+cdef class FTStringBracketState:
+    cdef int bracket_nesting_level

comment_data.body: There is active PR for that so I removed the annotation.

     def generate_object_constant_decls(self):
-        consts = [(len(c.cname), c.cname, c)
-                  for c in self.arg_default_constants]
